summary: Matched a large batch of short Kirby init/special-N related functions in ftKb_Init, targeting functions under 60 bytes. The PR replaced many nonmatching /// # stubs with small direct wrappers, accessors, flag setters/clearers, IASA handlers, collision callbacks, and simple physics helpers. It also tightened ftKb_Init.h prototypes, refined ftKb_FighterVars fields used by the new matches, replaced some magic fighter-kind indices with FTKIND_* constants, and fixed tools/easy_funcs.py for the PrettyTable TableStyle API. decomp.dev reported 62 new matches and +2708 matched bytes.

key_files.role: Main matching work: 403 added / 73 deleted lines across 29 hunks, replacing many ftKb_Init stubs with matching C implementations.

Prototype cleanup for newly matched functions and helper callbacks, including concrete signatures for ftKb_SpecialN_800EF69C, ftKb_SpecialNSk_80105FF0, ftKb_SpecialNYs helpers, and many fn_800F64xx collision callbacks.
Refined ftKb_FighterVars layout by splitting padding into x74/x78 fields and typing xB0/xB8 as u32 to support flag-based matches.
Tooling compatibility fix: changed prettytable.PLAIN_COLUMNS to prettytable.TableStyle.PLAIN_COLUMNS.

what_changed: Implemented many short ftKb_Init.c functions that were previously marked as nonmatching stubs, including copy-ability setup helpers, collision wrappers, physics helpers, simple getters, and flag tests/resets.
Added includes for common fighter behavior and collision helpers used by the new matches: ftCo_Jump.h, ftCo_KneeBend.h, ftwalkcommon.h, mp/mpcoll.h, and ftCo_Wait.h.
Added a default: break case in ftKb_Init_UnkMotionStates4, likely to match switch code generation.
Replaced magic hat/data indices with named fighter-kind constants in several places, for example FTKIND_PICHU, FTKIND_CAPTAIN, FTKIND_DRMARIO, and FTKIND_KIRBY.
Matched simple hat/copy-ability dispatch wrappers such as ftKb_SpecialN_800F10A4 and ftKb_SpecialN_800F13F0 by forwarding to ftKb_SpecialN_800EF69C with the correct hat table entry.

Matched many Kirby SpecialN collision callbacks as one-line wrappers around ft_8008403C for grounded transitions or ft_80082C74 for aerial transitions, passing the appropriate fn_800F64xx/fn_800F69xx callback.
Matched command-variable-gated IASA handlers for Mr. Game & Watch and Luigi copy abilities, calling ftCo_Wait_IASA on ground and ftCo_Fall_IASA_Inner in air.
Matched Fox copy ability IASA functions that set ft->mv.kb.specialhi.x0 when cmd_vars[0] is set and B is pressed.
Matched Ness, Pichu/Purin, Zelda/Sheik/Mewtwo/Yoshi-style copy-ability helpers, including basic physics, charge state, velocity vector, and attribute accessor functions.

Converted several Yoshi-copy attribute getters from M2C_FIELD offset reads to typed ftKb_DatAttrs field access, including specialn_ys_damage_multiplier, growth/base duration, breakout resistance, frames reduced per input, iframes on release, breakout velocity, and related unknown fields.
Updated ftKb_Init.h function signatures from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or incomplete prototypes to concrete return types and parameters where evidence was available.
Updated ftKb_FighterVars layout to expose x74, x78, xB0, and xB8 as integer/flag fields used by Link/Fox/Sheik-style helper matches.
Fixed easy_funcs.py to use prettytable.TableStyle.PLAIN_COLUMNS instead of prettytable.PLAIN_COLUMNS.
smart_moves: Focused on all functions under 60 bytes, which yielded many low-risk matches with high aggregate matched-byte gain.
Recognized repeated ground-vs-air collision wrapper patterns: ft_8008403C for ground callbacks and ft_80082C74 for air callbacks.

Reused existing common fighter routines instead of open-coding behavior: ftCo_Wait_IASA, ftCo_Fall_IASA_Inner, ftCommon_Fall, ft_80084F3C, ftColl_8007AEF8, mpColl_8004CBE8, efLib_DestroyAll, and ftParts helpers.
Improved readability and future maintainability by replacing raw fighter-kind numbers and M2C_FIELD offset access with FTKIND_* constants and named ftKb_DatAttrs fields.
Used explicit header prototypes to make newly matched callback signatures searchable and type-checked.
Split struct padding only where new matches needed concrete fields, avoiding an overbroad rewrite of ftKb_FighterVars.
Preserved matching quirks where needed, such as PAD_STACK(4) in Mewtwo copy-charge loop-full animation helpers and tiny nonzero horizontal velocity 0.0001f * facing_dir in Purin copy ground physics.
decomp_lessons: For mass matching very small functions, look for repeated wrappers around common transition/collision helpers before attempting more complex control flow.
Short copy-ability functions often reduce to setting/clearing one fighter variable, checking cmd_vars/input, or returning a dat_attrs field.
Replacing M2C_FIELD with named dat_attrs fields can both improve source quality and unlock matches once the struct layout is sufficiently understood.
Do not leave magic fighter-kind table indices in newly cleaned-up code when FTKIND_* constants exist; constants make hat-table and data-list accesses more reviewable.
Header accuracy matters for matching work: correcting return types and parameters can enable simple function bodies and reduce UNK_PARAMS propagation.
When a switch is almost matched, an explicit default: break can be necessary to reproduce compiler output even if it is behaviorally inert.
Expose only the struct fields proven by current use sites; here x74/x78/xB0/xB8 were introduced because the matched functions read or clear those flags.
naming_conventions: Kirby functions use the ftKb_ prefix; internal callback-like functions retain address-style fn_800Fxxxx names when semantic names are not yet known.
Copy-ability suffixes encode the donor fighter or move family, such as Mr, Lg, Fx, Ns, Pr, Zd, Sk, Mt, Ys, Ms.
Ground and aerial variants are named in pairs, for example SpecialN_Coll vs SpecialAirN_Coll and SpecialNLoop_Coll vs SpecialAirNLoop_Coll.
Function declarations in ftKb_Init.h retain address comments like /* 0F6450 */ next to prototypes.
Fighter-kind constants are preferred over numeric indices for hat/data list lookup, e.g. FTKIND_KIRBY instead of gFtDataList[4].
Known Kirby attribute fields use descriptive specialn_* names in ftKb_DatAttrs; unresolved fighter-var fields remain offset-like x74, x78, xB0, xB8.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: One-line forwarding wrappers matched by identifying the callee and exact callback/function pointer argument.
Ground/air collision wrappers matched by selecting ft_8008403C or ft_80082C74 according to state and passing the correct transition callback.
Attribute accessor functions matched by mapping previous M2C_FIELD offsets to named ftKb_DatAttrs fields.
Boolean/flag helpers matched with explicit null checks and nested conditions rather than assuming GET_FIGHTER on nullable gobj, preserving observed control flow.
For vector-return helpers, fields were assigned in the observed order and z was explicitly zeroed.
PAD_STACK(4) was retained in two short Mewtwo charge functions as a stack-layout matching aid.
A tiny value, 0.0001f * facing_dir, was used in Purin copy ground physics helpers to match original velocity setup.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported matched code 45.69% (+0.07%, +2708 bytes) and 62 new matches in main/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_Init.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find other ftKirby functions under 60 bytes still using /// # stubs.
Search for remaining M2C_FIELD accesses in ftKb_Init.c that could be converted to ftKb_DatAttrs fields.
Audit remaining gFtDataList numeric indices in fighter code and replace with FTKIND_* constants where known.
Look for repeated ft_8008403C/ft_80082C74 collision wrapper patterns in other fighters.
Check whether ftKb_FighterVars fields x74, x78, xB0, and xB8 can be semantically renamed based on additional use sites.
